reasoning: The BC government explicitly banned Tesla products in mid-March 2025 as part of a trade retaliation strategy (linked to US tariffs), backed by an emergency powers bill. A quick resolution of the US-Canada trade dispute within just 3.5 months (by July 1, 2025) is possible but unlikely. Even if a broad federal trade agreement is reached before July, updating the exact text of the BC Hydro rebate page to remove the specific exclusion of Tesla is highly improbable due to bureaucratic inertia. Removing the specific mention would also cause consumer confusion since Tesla is a major brand in this space. Additionally, singling out Tesla (and by extension Elon Musk) is politically attractive for the B.C. NDP government, decreasing the likelihood of an early reversal. Therefore, I assign a high probability that the website will still specifically mention Tesla's ineligibility on July 1, 2025.
batch_queries: Will BC Hydro still exclude Tesla from its EV charger rebate program for single-family homes in July 2025?
